back_translation: Likewise, wives, submit to your own husbands, so that even if some do not obey the word, they may be persuaded to faith by the conduct of the wives without using words
when they have observed your pure conduct in reverence
Your beauty should not come from outward adornment, such as braided hairstyles, gold ornaments, or fine clothing
but from the inner self of the heart, in the imperishable quality of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is very precious in God’s sight
For in former times, the holy women who set their hope in God adorned themselves in this way, submitting to their own husbands
Just as Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him master. You have become her children if you do good and fear nothing at all
Husbands, likewise, live with your wives knowledgeably, honoring her as a weaker vessel, and as fellow heirs of the grace of life, so that your prayers will not be hindered
Finally, all of you, be of one heart-and-mind, sympathetic with one another, loving as brothers, having a kind heart, and humble
Do not repay evil with evil, or insult with insult; on the contrary, bless. For you were called to this, so that you may inherit a blessing
For, “Whoever desires to love life and to see good days, let him stop his tongue from evil and his lips from speaking deceitfully
Let him turn away from evil and do good; let him seek peace and pursue it
For the eyes of the Lord watch over the righteous, and His ears are inclined to listen to their prayers, but the face of the Lord opposes those who do evil”
Who can harm you if you have zeal for doing good?
But even if you must suffer for the sake of righteousness, you are blessed. “Do not fear what they fear, and do not be shaken”
But sanctify Christ as Lord in your heart. Always be ready to give a defense to everyone who asks the reason for the hope that is in you
But do this with gentleness and reverence, having a good conscience, so that in matters where they slander you, those who speak ill of your good conduct in Christ will be put to shame
For suffering because of doing good, if that is the will of God, is better than suffering because of doing evil
For Christ also suffered for sins once for all, the righteous one for the unrighteous, in order to bring you to God. He was put to death in the flesh but was made alive in the Spirit
In that Spirit, He went and proclaimed to the spirits in custody
who in former times had been disobedient, when the patience of God waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was being built. In that ark only a few people, namely eight, were saved through water

Baptism, which is a representation of that water, now also saves you — not the washing away of dirt from the body, but a pledge of a good conscience toward God, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ
who has ascended into heaven and sits at the right hand of God, with angels, authorities, and powers subjected to Him
